audit_items.proof_trace: The return value of malloc() at line 46 is never checked for NULL before values[] is first dereferenced at line 49 (and subsequently at lines 69, 70, 96, 97). All LMDB API calls are guarded by the E() macro which aborts on failure, but malloc is not, leaving a gap in the proof. External reachability via LDAP is incorrect — mtest5.c is a standalone test binary with no network I/O, so exploitation requires direct execution under memory pressure.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A process running mtest5 on a memory-constrained system receives NULL from malloc at line 46; the immediately following loop at line 49 (values[i] = rand()%1024) dereferences NULL, causing a crash. Direct LDAP attacker triggering is not possible as this is a standalone test utility.
